title: Drawdown-Proportional Position Scaler
summary: Implements a simple but powerful drawdown-aware position scaler that multiplies the final position size by risk_scaling = max(0, 1 - (current_drawdown / max_drawdown_threshold)). When the bot is at peak equity, risk_scaling = 1.0 (no reduction). At 10% drawdown with a 20% threshold, risk_scaling = 0.5 (half size). At the threshold, risk_scaling = 0 (no new positions). This is documented with working Python code in published practitioner research and is validated by Chekhlov et al.'s CDaR framework. It directly replaces the archived 'Drawdown Brake — Tiered Equity Curve Circuit Breaker' which used discrete tiers; this uses a continuous scalar that avoids cliff-edge behaviour.
expected_impact: Reduces catastrophic loss scenarios by linearly scaling new allocations to zero as drawdown approaches max threshold; practitioner code shows risk_scaling = 1 - (drawdown / max_drawdown), preserving capital during drawdown periods while avoiding the 'locking in losses' problem if max_drawdown threshold is set generously (e.g., 20%)
